# Lumina Protocol — Full LLM Reference

> Manual override of `/llms-full.txt`. Mintlify's auto-generator was
> serving a stale OpenAPI sample instead of the canonical Lumina spec at
> `/openapi.json`. This file is the source-of-truth machine-readable
> summary of the Lumina docs. Source pages live at
> https://docs.lumina-org.com.
>
> Build trigger: 2026-05-06 v3 (cache-bust to force Mintlify rebuild).
> Last updated: 2026-05-06

---

## About

Lumina Protocol is parametric DeFi insurance built for AI agents. It runs
on Base Sepolia (chainId 84532) today, with Base mainnet rollout gated on
audit-fix completion. Three properties make it easier to integrate than
typical DeFi:

- **Programmatic.** Buy a policy in one HTTP call. No KYC, no docs to sign,
  no broker.
- **Relayer-paid.** Agents do not pay gas. The off-chain relayer signs
  `purchasePolicyFor` on behalf of the buyer; the agent only holds USDC for
  premiums.
- **Fair payouts.** Triggers come from a signed off-chain oracle and pay out
  as ERC-1155 bonds the agent can hold, redeem at maturity, or sell on a
  secondary marketplace.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com

---

## Quick Discovery

Canonical public URLs an autonomous agent should know about:

| Resource | URL |
|---|---|
| Homepage | https://www.lumina-org.com |
| Docs | https://docs.lumina-org.com |
| App (UI for humans) | https://www.lumina-org.com/app |
| Self-service API key issuance | https://www.lumina-org.com/app/agent/api-keys |
| API base URL | https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app |
| Health (live contract addresses) | https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/health |
| OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec | https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/openapi.json |
| Swagger UI | https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/api-docs |
| Sandbox info | https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/sandbox/info |
| npm SDK | https://www.npmjs.com/package/@lumina-org/sdk |
| GitHub org | https://github.com/org-lumina |
| SDK source | https://github.com/org-lumina/lumina-sdk |
| Docs source | https://github.com/org-lumina/docs |
| Postman collection | https://github.com/org-lumina/lumina-api/blob/main/docs/lumina-api.postman_collection.json |

`/health` is the canonical source for live contract addresses on every
deploy. Always pull from it on boot rather than hardcoding addresses — a
redeploy invalidates yesterday's `.env`.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/contracts/deployed and
https://docs.lumina-org.com/status

---

## Quickstart (5 minutes)

The shortest path from zero to a real on-chain policy. Five minutes if your
wallet is funded with Base Sepolia ETH for the onboarding signature only —
the protocol itself never asks the agent for gas.

### 1. Install the SDK

```bash
npm install @lumina-org/sdk ethers
# or pnpm add / yarn add
```

`ethers` v6 is a peer-ish dependency, used for asset bytes32 encoding and
the `Signer` type accepted by `agent.onboard(signer)`.

### 2. Mint an API key (self-service)

Sign one canonical message with your wallet — the API verifies the
signature recovers to the wallet address and returns a fresh `lk_…` key.

```ts
import { Wallet } from 'ethers'
import { LuminaClient } from '@lumina-org/sdk'

const wallet = new Wallet(process.env.PRIVATE_KEY!)
const lumina = new LuminaClient({ apiKey: '' })  // empty for onboard

const { apiKey } = await lumina.agent.onboard(wallet, { label: 'my-bot' })
console.log('Save this NOW (shown only once):', apiKey)
```

Canonical message: `Lumina onboarding for {address} at {timestamp}`. The
SDK builds it for you. Caps: 3 active keys per wallet, 10 onboarding
attempts per hour per IP, ±300 second timestamp window.

### 3. Verify connectivity

```ts
const lumina = new LuminaClient({ apiKey: process.env.LUMINA_API_KEY! })
const health = await lumina.health()
console.log('chainId:', health.chain.chainId)              // 84532
console.log('contracts:', Object.keys(health.contracts))   // coverRouter, …
```

### 4. Buy a $50 policy

```ts
const policy = await lumina.policies.purchase({
  productName: 'FLASHBTC1H-001',  // SDK 0.3.0+ auto-resolves productId hash + asset='BTC'
  buyer: '0xYourWalletAddress',   // wallet that pays the USDC premium
  coverageAmount: '50000000',     // $50 in 6-dec USDC base units
})
console.log('policyId:', policy.policyId, 'tx:', policy.txHash)
```

The relayer paid the gas, your buyer wallet paid the USDC premium, and you
got back a `policyId` to reference for triggers and bonds.

### 5. Demo without an account

```bash
curl -X POST https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/sandbox/try
```

Server-fixed buyer wallet, fixed `$1` coverage. Useful for first-call
demos. 10 requests per hour per IP.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/quickstart

---

## How Lumina Works

Four moving parts. Once you know them, every endpoint in the API is just a
verb on one of these nouns.

```
        Buyer (wallet/agent)
               │  premium (USDC)
               ▼
   ┌─────────────────────────┐
   │      CoverRouterV2      │ ◄──── Relayer pays gas (purchasePolicyFor)
   └────────┬────────────────┘
            ▼
   ┌─────────────────────────┐
   │     PolicyManagerV2     │ records policy, snapshots LUMINA price
   └────────┬────────────────┘
            ▼
   ┌─────────────────────────┐
   │   Shield (per product)  │ verifies oracle signature, checks condition
   └────────┬────────────────┘
            ▼  (when triggered)
   ┌─────────────────────────┐
   │       BondVault         │ mints ERC-1155 bond to buyer
   └────────┬────────────────┘
            ▼
   ┌─────────────────────────┐
   │      ClaimBond          │ holdable, redeemable, sellable in marketplace
   └─────────────────────────┘
```

| Concept | What it is |
|---|---|
| Shield | A specific parametric product (e.g. "BTC drops 20% in 1h"). Each Shield is its own contract; `Shield.submitTrigger(...)` accepts an EIP-712 signed price proof from the oracle and decides whether the condition is met. |
| Policy | The buyer's coverage. Stored in `PolicyManagerV2`, indexed by `(productId, policyId)`. Each policy snapshots the LUMINA/USD price at purchase time so payouts use a price the buyer accepted. |
| Trigger | The off-chain oracle's claim that the condition fired, signed with EIP-712. Anyone can submit it; the Shield verifies and only mints the bond if the condition holds. |
| Claim Bond | The payout. ERC-1155 token issued by `BondVault`. The agent can hold it, redeem it for USDC at maturity, or list it on the secondary marketplace. |

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/overview

---

## Products and Assets

Lumina distinguishes two assets per product:

- **coveredAsset** — the asset whose price-event the policy insures
  against. This is what each shield contract validates `params.asset`
  against. Sending the wrong value reverts with `InvalidAsset(bytes32)`
  (selector `0x8196d462`).
- **paymentAsset** — the token used to pay the premium and receive
  payouts. **Always USDC** for every product.

Both fields are exposed by `GET /products`. The bytes32 hashes below come
from the live `/products` endpoint and match the `PRODUCT_ID` constants
in each `Shield*.sol` on Base Sepolia.

| Product name | coveredAsset | paymentAsset | Shield type | Duration | productId (truncated) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `FLASHBTC1H-001` | `BTC` | `USDC` | Flash crash on BTC | 1 h | `0xe87625ef…dfeaddd` |
| `FLASHBTC4H-001` | `BTC` | `USDC` | Flash crash on BTC | 4 h | `0x0c8e45ca…a03d` |
| `FLASHBTC24-001` | `BTC` | `USDC` | Flash crash on BTC | 24 h | `0xdc5bcc7d…a7b9` |
| `FLASHBTC48-001` | `BTC` | `USDC` | Flash crash on BTC | 48 h | `0xb6306087…1ee8` |
| `FLASHETH1H-001` | `ETH` | `USDC` | Flash crash on ETH | 1 h | `0x6cedbccf…8915` |
| `FLASHETH24-001` | `ETH` | `USDC` | Flash crash on ETH | 24 h | `0xcc03aef9…f240` |
| `FLASHETH48-001` | `ETH` | `USDC` | Flash crash on ETH | 48 h | `0x89a37df7…712d` |
| `MICRODEPEG-001` | `USDT` | `USDC` | Stablecoin micro-depeg | 7 d | `0x317c1a64…3640` |
| `RATESHOCK-001` | `USDC` | `USDC` | Borrow-rate spike on Aave V3 | 7 d | `0x8ae1e414…347f` |

### Recommended pattern: pass `productName`

SDK 0.3.0 and the `POST /api/v1/policies` endpoint both auto-resolve the
bytes32 productId hash AND the per-shield asset literal from
`productName`. You don't need to compute keccak yourself, and you can't
accidentally pair `FLASHBTC1H-001` with `USDC`.

```ts
const policy = await lumina.policies.purchase({
  productName: 'FLASHBTC1H-001',  // resolves productId + asset='BTC'
  buyer: '0xYourWalletAddress',
  coverageAmount: '50000000',     // $50, paid in USDC
})
```

### Common errors

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `InvalidAsset(bytes32)` | Sent the wrong asset for that shield | Use `productName` and let the resolver pick the asset |
| `unknown_product` (400) | productName not in the registry | Only the 9 listed products are deployed |
| `ProductNotConfigured` | productId hash doesn't exist on-chain | Verify the keccak256 preimage matches a canonical name |
| `InvalidCoverage` | Below `$100` minimum (`MIN_PRICE_FOR_NEW_POLICIES`) | Increase `coverageAmount` to ≥ `100000000` |

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/agents/products-and-assets,
https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/shields, and
https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/assets (covered vs payment asset)

---

## Deployed Contracts (Base Sepolia, chainId 84532)

Snapshot on **2026-05-05**. The single source of truth is `/health` —
never hardcode these in production:

| Contract | Address (verify against /health) |
|---|---|
| coverRouter | `0xebC3A783477FbD2720C024e16A8d63B8Db983D84` |
| policyManager | `0xd9732A8d6Cf5266Dd896B825E78E387B7Dd2c379` |
| bondVault | `0x101F92fC506C1e60A2A0dD01eA29597EBf222d2B` |
| claimBond | `0x3d2F5DB2505367D00ef81c51AD3cA66159271730` |
| marketplace | `0xfaC56692c626718aC8953A3d5fAE67fac2f1Be6E` |
| usdc (mock) | `0xD944d8e5D8329994D83950872Ec210891d3Ab6AE` |
| luminaToken | `0x8A0FDc2126eb9b0c88D17711D62713A1c06CF7Ab` |

Mainnet not yet deployed — gated on completion of audit fixes.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/contracts/deployed

---

## API Reference

The Lumina API is a thin REST surface over the on-chain protocol. The
canonical contract is the OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec at `/openapi.json`. This
section is a human-friendly tour of the most-used endpoints.

### Base URL

```
https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app
```

Local dev defaults to `http://localhost:3000` (override with `PORT` env in
the `lumina-api` repo).

### Authentication

| Surface | Auth |
|---|---|
| `/health`, `/products`, `/policies/:p/:i`, `/sandbox/*`, `/openapi.json`, `/api-docs` | None |
| `/api/v1/agent/onboard` | None — request body itself is signed by the wallet |
| `/api/v1/policies`, `/bonds`, `/marketplace/listings`, `/agent/keys`, `/webhooks` | `x-api-key: lk_…` |
| `/api/v1/keys/generate` (legacy admin issuer) | `x-admin-token: <secret>` |

### Numeric encoding

Every on-chain integer (USDC base units, LUMINA wei, prices) is returned
as a **decimal string** to avoid JavaScript Number precision loss. Convert
with `BigInt(str)`.

### Error envelope

```json
{ "error": "machine_code", "message": "human-readable", "code": "machine_code" }
```

The SDK maps non-2xx into `LuminaError` with `.status`, `.code`, `.message`.

### Idempotency

`POST /api/v1/policies` honours an optional `Idempotency-Key` header
(UUIDv4 recommended). Replays return the cached response without
double-spending — safe to retry on network blips.

### Endpoint: GET /health

Returns the canonical contract address set, the relayer wallet, and chain
status. Public — no key required.

```bash
curl https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/health
```

Response fields: `status` (`ok` / `degraded`), `chain.chainId` (84532),
`chain.block`, `chain.rpcConnected`, `relayer.address`,
`relayer.balanceWei`, `contracts` (map).

### Endpoint: GET /products

Public list of registered shields, their productIds, durations,
`payoutRatioBps`, and `triggerProbBps`.

### Endpoint: POST /api/v1/policies (purchase a policy)

Buy parametric coverage on behalf of `buyer`. Relayer signs
`purchasePolicyFor` and pays gas; buyer pays USDC premium.

```bash
curl -X POST https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/api/v1/policies \
  -H "x-api-key: $LUMINA_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Idempotency-Key: $(uuidgen)" \
  -d '{
    "productName":    "FLASHBTC1H-001",
    "coverageAmount": "50000000",
    "buyer":          "0xYourWalletAddress"
  }'
```

Body fields:

- **`productName`** — canonical name. Preferred input; the API resolves
  both `productId` hash and per-shield asset literal from it.
- **`productId`** — bytes32 hash. Optional alternative to `productName`.
- **`coverageAmount`** — string of USDC base units. `$50 = "50000000"`.
- **`asset`** — bytes32 of asset symbol. **Optional** — auto-resolved from
  the registry. Must equal the product's `coveredAsset` (NOT the USDC
  payment token). Sending the wrong value reverts `InvalidAsset(bytes32)`
  (selector `0x8196d462`). See
  https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/assets for the distinction.
- **`buyer`** — wallet that pays USDC premium. Must hold ≥ premium and
  have approved the relayer-side spender.

Response: `{ policyId, txHash, blockNumber, buyer, productId,
coverageAmount, premiumPaid }`.

Errors:

| HTTP | Code | Retry? |
|---|---|---|
| 400 | `validation_error` | No |
| 401 | `invalid_api_key` | No |
| 422 | `shield_paused` | Maybe later |
| 422 | `exceeds_capacity` | Maybe later |
| 429 | `rate_limit` | Yes (backoff) |
| 5xx | `server_error` | Yes (≤3 attempts) |

### Endpoint: GET /policies/{productId}/{policyId}

Public read of a single policy. No auth required.

### Endpoint: GET /api/v1/policies

Lists policies for the calling wallet. Cross-wallet listing returns 403.

### Endpoint: POST /api/v1/agent/onboard

Self-service API key issuance. The body itself is signed by the wallet —
no auth header. Caps: 3 active keys per wallet, 10 attempts per hour per
IP, ±300 second timestamp window.

Body: `{ walletAddress, signature, timestamp, label }`. The signed
canonical message is `Lumina onboarding for {address} at {timestamp}`
(EIP-191 personal_sign).

Errors: `invalid_body` (400), `stale_timestamp` (400),
`invalid_signature` (401), `cap_reached` (409), `rate_limit` (429).

### Endpoint: GET / DELETE /api/v1/agent/keys

List metadata (never plaintext) and revoke keys owned by the calling
wallet.

### Endpoint: POST /api/v1/webhooks

Subscribe a URL to receive HMAC-signed event deliveries. See the Webhooks
section below.

### Endpoint: GET / DELETE /api/v1/webhooks

List subscriptions (metadata only — secret is never returned again) and
delete by id.

### Endpoint: GET /api/v1/bonds

List ERC-1155 bond positions for the calling wallet.

### Endpoint: GET /api/v1/marketplace/listings

Browse active secondary-market listings. Query parameters: `limit`
(default 50), `offset` (default 0), `sortBy`
(`price-asc | price-desc | createdAt-desc | listedAt-desc`),
`maxPriceUsdc`.

### Endpoint: GET /sandbox/info

Returns sandbox status, server-funded wallet address, coverage cap,
default `productName`, rate limits.

### Endpoint: POST /sandbox/try

Execute a real on-chain policy purchase using a shared, server-funded
wallet. No API key required. 10 requests per IP per hour. Coverage is
fixed at the on-chain minimum (`coverageCapUsdc`, default $100). Bonds
minted by sandbox triggers belong to the shared wallet, not the caller.

```bash
curl -X POST https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/sandbox/try \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"productName":"MICRODEPEG-001"}'
```

### Endpoint: POST /api/v1/policies/{id}/trigger

Convenience wrapper that asks the oracle for a fresh EIP-712 proof and
calls `Shield.submitTrigger(payload, signature)` for the policy. Anyone
can submit — the signature is what gives the trigger weight.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/api-reference/introduction

---

## SDK — `@lumina-org/sdk`

Current published version: **0.3.0** on npm. TypeScript-first; types are
bundled (no `@types/lumina-org__sdk`). Node 18+ required (uses global
`fetch`).

```bash
npm install @lumina-org/sdk ethers
```

### Construction

```ts
import { LuminaClient } from '@lumina-org/sdk'

const lumina = new LuminaClient({
  apiKey: process.env.LUMINA_API_KEY!,
  baseUrl: 'https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app',  // default
})
```

Construction is synchronous and never makes a network call. Call
`.health()` to verify reachability.

### Sub-resources

| Property | Endpoints |
|---|---|
| `lumina.products` | `GET /products`, `GET /products/:id`, `GET /products/:id/quote` |
| `lumina.policies` | `GET / POST /api/v1/policies`, `GET /policies/:prod/:id` |
| `lumina.bonds` | `GET /api/v1/bonds` |
| `lumina.marketplace` | `GET /api/v1/marketplace/listings` |
| `lumina.agent` | `POST /api/v1/agent/onboard`, `GET / DELETE /api/v1/agent/keys` |
| `lumina.webhooks` | `POST / GET / DELETE /api/v1/webhooks` |
| `lumina.sandbox` | `GET /sandbox/info`, `POST /sandbox/try` |

### Errors

```ts
import { LuminaError } from '@lumina-org/sdk'

try {
  await lumina.policies.purchase({ … })
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof LuminaError) {
    err.status   // HTTP status code
    err.code     // e.g. 'invalid_api_key'
    err.message  // human-readable
  }
}
```

Network-level errors (connection refused, timeout) are also wrapped as
`LuminaError` with `status === 0` and `code === 'network_error'`.

### Buy a policy with retry-on-blip

```ts
import { LuminaClient, LuminaError } from '@lumina-org/sdk'
import { randomUUID } from 'crypto'

const lumina = new LuminaClient({ apiKey: process.env.LUMINA_API_KEY! })
const idempotencyKey = randomUUID()

async function buyOnce(): Promise<string> {
  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 3; attempt++) {
    try {
      const r = await lumina.policies.purchase({
        productName: 'FLASHBTC24-001',
        buyer: process.env.BUYER!,
        coverageAmount: '50000000',
        idempotencyKey,
      })
      return r.policyId
    } catch (err) {
      if (!(err instanceof LuminaError)) throw err
      if (err.status >= 500 || err.code === 'network_error') {
        await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 1000 * Math.pow(2, attempt)))
        continue
      }
      throw err   // 4xx → bug; do not retry
    }
  }
  throw new Error('giving up after 3 attempts')
}
```

### Read bonds and marketplace

```ts
const bonds = await lumina.bonds.list()
//   [{ bondId, owner, amount, faceValueUsdc, pricePerBondUsdc?, maturityEpoch? }]

const listings = await lumina.marketplace.listings({
  limit: 50,
  sortBy: 'price-asc',
  maxPriceUsdc: '5000000000',
})
```

### Asset encoding (advanced override)

```ts
asset: 'BTC'    // encoded to 0x4254430000…0000 for you
asset: '0x5553444300000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000'
```

The SDK accepts symbols (`'BTC' | 'ETH' | 'USDC' | 'USDT'`) and encodes
them to bytes32, or a 32-byte hex string directly. Recommended: don't
override — pass `productName` and let the SDK auto-resolve from the
registry.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/sdk/installation,
https://docs.lumina-org.com/sdk/client,
https://docs.lumina-org.com/sdk/policies,
https://docs.lumina-org.com/sdk/bonds,
https://docs.lumina-org.com/sdk/examples

---

## Webhooks — HMAC delivery semantics

Push notifications for policy / bond / marketplace events. HMAC-signed.
Polling is wasteful — subscribe a URL and the API will POST JSON when
something happens.

### Subscribe

```ts
const sub = await lumina.webhooks.create({
  url: 'https://my-bot.example.com/webhooks/lumina',
  events: ['policy_purchased', 'policy_triggered', 'bond_minted'],
})
console.log('STORE THIS NOW:', sub.secret)  // 32-byte hex, returned ONCE
```

The `secret` is a 32-byte hex string. Returned exactly once. Store it in a
secret manager — there is no API to retrieve it later.

### Available events

| Event | Fires when |
|---|---|
| `policy_purchased` | A `purchasePolicyFor` call completes successfully |
| `policy_triggered` | A trigger is accepted on-chain and a bond is minted |
| `bond_minted` | Same trigger event, surfaced from the BondVault perspective |
| `bond_redeemed` | A bond is redeemed for USDC at maturity |
| `listing_created` | Your wallet listed a bond on the marketplace |
| `listing_purchased` | Your listing was bought by another wallet |

Use `events: '*'` to subscribe to everything for the wallet (recommended
for new integrations).

### Headers on every delivery

| Header | Value |
|---|---|
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |
| `X-Lumina-Signature` | `hex(HMAC-SHA256(rawBody, secret))` |
| `X-Lumina-Event` | The event name, e.g. `policy_purchased` |
| `X-Lumina-Delivery` | A unique delivery ID for de-duplication |

### Verifying the signature (Express example)

```ts
import crypto from 'crypto'
import express from 'express'

const app = express()

// IMPORTANT: use raw body, not the JSON-parsed one — the HMAC is over the
// exact bytes the API sent.
app.post('/webhooks/lumina', express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }), (req, res) => {
  const sig = req.header('X-Lumina-Signature') ?? ''
  const expected = crypto.createHmac('sha256', process.env.LUMINA_WEBHOOK_SECRET!)
    .update(req.body)
    .digest('hex')

  if (!crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(sig), Buffer.from(expected))) {
    return res.status(401).send('bad signature')
  }

  const payload = JSON.parse(req.body.toString('utf8'))
  console.log('event:', req.header('X-Lumina-Event'), payload)
  res.sendStatus(200)
})
```

### Delivery semantics

- **Acceptance:** any 2xx response counts as delivered.
- **4xx:** marked failed permanently (the receiver said no).
- **5xx / network:** retried with exponential backoff (30s · 2^attempt).
- **Max attempts:** 3 (longest gap from event to final failure ≈150s).

The worker polls the queue every 30 seconds. Latency from event to first
delivery attempt is therefore 0–30s.

### Limits

- **One URL per wallet.** Re-registering the same `(wallet, url)` pair
  returns 409. Delete and re-register to rotate the secret.
- **Multiple URLs allowed.** Different URLs (dev/staging/prod) can coexist
  on the same wallet.
- **HTTPS required** in production. `http://localhost` is allowed for
  testing.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/agents/webhooks

---

## Triggers and Claim Bonds (payout flow)

When a policy's parametric condition fires, the on-chain payout flow is
fully deterministic.

### What's signed (EIP-712)

```
struct PriceProof {
  bytes32 asset;      // keccak256("BTC"), etc.
  uint256 price;      // 18 decimals
  uint256 timestamp;  // unix seconds; ≤ MAX_PROOF_AGE old
  uint256 nonce;      // per-asset replay counter
}
```

Shield verifies:

1. Signature recovers to `LuminaOracleV2.oracleKey()` — the only allowed
   signer.
2. `block.timestamp - payload.timestamp ≤ MAX_PROOF_AGE` (24 hours after
   audit fix M-8).
3. Nonce is strictly greater than the last accepted nonce for the asset
   (replay protection).
4. Asset/price meets the Shield's specific condition.

### Claim Bond

The ERC-1155 token issued by `BondVault` when a Shield triggers. Three
things you can do with it:

1. **Hold and redeem.** Wait until maturity, call `BondVault.redeem(epochId)`
   to exchange for USDC.
2. **List on the marketplace.** Sell at any time, usually for a small
   discount to face value.
3. **Buy more.** Snipe other people's listings priced below face value.

Token id is the bond's epoch id — bonds with the same epoch share a
maturity date. `BondIssued(buyer, epochId, amount)` is emitted at mint
time.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/triggers and
https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/claimbonds

---

## Marketplace

Secondary market for `ClaimBond` ERC-1155 tokens. Sellers exit early in
USDC; buyers redeem at maturity (730d) for `$LUMINA`.

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Contract (Base Sepolia) | `0xfaC56692c626718aC8953A3d5fAE67fac2f1Be6E` |
| Maker fee | `150 bps` (1.5%) |
| Taker fee | `150 bps` (1.5%) |
| Total fee per fill | `3%` |
| Anti-spam floor | `minPricePerUnit = $1` (1_000_000 raw, 6-dec USDC) |
| Settlement asset | USDC (6-dec) |
| Redemption asset (at maturity) | `$LUMINA` |

### API endpoints

- `GET  /api/v1/marketplace/listings`
- `POST /api/v1/marketplace/list`
- `POST /api/v1/marketplace/buy`
- `GET  /api/v1/marketplace/stats`
- `GET  /api/v1/marketplace/history`
- `GET  /api/v1/marketplace/listings/:id`

### SDK methods (`@lumina-org/sdk` 0.5.0)

`marketplace.listings`, `marketplace.listing`, `marketplace.stats`,
`marketplace.history`, `marketplace.myListings`, `marketplace.list`,
`marketplace.buy`, `marketplace.cancel`, `marketplace.approve`,
`marketplace.approveBonds`.

### Lifecycle (6 steps)

1. Policy bought (USDC premium).
2. Trigger fires → `ClaimBond` ERC-1155 minted to buyer.
3. Seller calls `approveBonds()` (one-time) and
   `list({ bondId, amount, pricePerUnit, expiresAt })`.
4. Buyer browses `listings()` / `listing(id)` / `estimateBuyPrice`,
   approves USDC via `approve(usdcAmount)`, then `buy({ listingId, amount })`.
5. Seller receives USDC (`3%` fee deducted across maker + taker).
6. Buyer holds bond until maturity (730d) → calls `redeemBond` for
   `$LUMINA`.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/concepts/marketplace

---

## Onboarding flow (wallet-signed key issuance)

API keys are minted via signature, not via a request to a human admin.

1. Build the canonical message: `Lumina onboarding for {address} at {timestamp}`.
2. Sign with your wallet (EIP-191 `personal_sign`).
3. POST `{walletAddress, signature, timestamp, label}` to
   `/api/v1/agent/onboard`.
4. The API verifies the signature recovers to `walletAddress`, then mints
   a key.
5. Plaintext key is in the response body — store it now, never returned
   again.

### curl form

```bash
ADDRESS=0xYourWalletAddress
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)
MESSAGE="Lumina onboarding for ${ADDRESS} at ${TIMESTAMP}"
SIGNATURE=$(cast wallet sign "$MESSAGE" --private-key "$PRIVATE_KEY")

curl -X POST https://lumina-api-production-ac85.up.railway.app/api/v1/agent/onboard \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"walletAddress\":\"${ADDRESS}\",\"signature\":\"${SIGNATURE}\",\"timestamp\":${TIMESTAMP},\"label\":\"my-bot\"}"
```

### Caps and limits

| Limit | Value |
|---|---|
| Active keys per wallet | 3 (revoke before issuing the 4th) |
| Onboarding requests per hour per IP | 10 |
| Timestamp window | ±300 seconds of server time |

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/agents/onboarding

---

## Status / monitoring

```ts
import { LuminaClient } from '@lumina-org/sdk'

const lumina = new LuminaClient({ apiKey: '' })
const h = await lumina.health()

if (!h.chain.rpcConnected) alert('RPC down')
if (BigInt(h.relayer.balanceWei) < 5_000_000_000_000_000n) alert('relayer low gas')
```

Field meanings:

- `status` — `ok` if processing requests; `degraded` otherwise.
- `chain.chainId` — always 84532 on the current deploy.
- `chain.block` — most recent block observed via RPC.
- `chain.rpcConnected` — `false` means upstream RPC is unreachable; buys
  and triggers will start failing soon.
- `relayer.address` — wallet that signs `purchasePolicyFor`.
- `relayer.balanceWei` — gas budget; below ~0.005 ETH means re-fund
  overdue.
- `contracts` — map of canonical contract addresses.

Source: https://docs.lumina-org.com/status

---
